headline: What is Jevons Paradox and why does more AI mean more labor?
summary: Jevons Paradox outlines that increased efficiency in resource usage often leads to higher — rather than lower — total consumption. So what does that mean for the big push into AI for industries outside of Big Tech? Morning Brief Host Julie Hyman Barron's Investor Circle Newsletter editor Josh Schafer relate this economic theory to the AI Boom and what the pivot toward the "everything is AI now" philosophy has meant for AI consumption and labor demands.

content.title: What is Jevons Paradox and why does more AI mean more labor?
